quick_facts.label: Program format
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. Nursing is a four-year, full-time undergraduate nursing program organised across eight semesters under the Indian Nursing Council (INC) revised curriculum.

quick_facts.label: Baseline entry eligibility
quick_facts.value: INC lists 10+2 Science with Physics, Chemistry and Biology (PCB), plus English Core/Elective, with 45% aggregate marks; applicants must be at least 17 by 31 December of the admission year and medically fit.

quick_facts.label: 2026–27 admission calendar
quick_facts.value: For academic year 2026–27, INC notified that the entrance test should be conducted before 15 June 2026, the academic session begins 1 August 2026, and the last admission date is 30 September 2026.

quick_facts.label: Professional progression
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. Nursing is an accepted qualification for INC-listed M.Sc. Nursing entry, subject to registration, marks and experience requirements; it is also an accepted degree route in AIIMS Nursing Officer recruitment notices when paired with nurse-and-midwife registration.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: The INC’s baseline eligibility for Basic B.Sc. Nursing is passing 10+2 with Science (Physics, Chemistry and Biology) and English Core or English Elective, with 45% aggregate marks from a recognised board. The applicant must be medically fit and must be 17 years old by 31 December of the year in which admission is sought. INC also lists eligible National Institute of Open School (NIOS) science candidates with 45% marks.

Admission authorities, universities and state counselling bodies can apply further conditions, including their own entrance-test, domicile, reservation, document-verification and category rules. Applicants should therefore use the applicable 2026–27 prospectus in addition to checking the INC baseline.
bullets: 10+2 Science: PCB plus English Core/Elective.
Minimum stated aggregate: 45% in the qualifying examination.
Minimum age: 17 years by 31 December of the admission year.
Medical fitness is required.

sections.heading: Duration and academic calendar
paragraphs: The revised INC B.Sc. Nursing curriculum describes a four-year full-time program comprising eight semesters. The curriculum uses a semester and credit-system structure adapted for professional education.

For the 2026–27 intake, INC scheduled commencement on 1 August 2026 and set 30 September 2026 as the last date of admission. These are academic-cycle dates, not an individual college’s application deadline; candidates must check the relevant state or university admission process.
bullets: Normal program length: 4 years.
Academic structure: 8 semesters.
2026–27 INC session commencement: 1 August 2026.
2026–27 INC last admission date: 30 September 2026.

sections.heading: Curriculum and training
paragraphs: The INC revised syllabus is competency- and outcome-based. It combines foundational, core and elective courses; electives are offered as modules, while modular learning is integrated into mandatory foundational and core learning. The framework is designed to develop clinical care strategies, critical thinking, communication, teamwork, safety, quality and evidence-based practice.

Learning is not limited to classroom theory. The prescribed framework includes laboratory and clinical learning, with subjects spanning applied anatomy and physiology, psychology and sociology, nutrition and biochemistry, microbiology and infection control, nursing foundations, pharmacology, pathology, genetics, medical-surgical nursing, child health, mental-health nursing, midwifery and obstetric nursing, community-health nursing, nursing management, research and statistics.
bullets: Foundational, core and elective courses under a semester-credit format.
Theory, skills-laboratory and clinical/practicum components.
Competency focus: safe care, teamwork, communication, quality and evidence-based practice.
The 2022 INC corrigendum updated elements of admission, curriculum and examination provisions in the 2021 regulations.

sections.heading: Admission route
paragraphs: For academic year 2026–27, INC states that admissions to B.Sc. Nursing are to be through a common entrance examination conducted by a State Government Common Entrance Cell or universities, with the B.Sc. Nursing syllabus admission terms and entrance criteria to be followed. The INC notice set 15 June 2026 as the date before which that entrance test should be conducted.

This means applicants should not assume a single all-India admission mechanism for every B.Sc. Nursing seat. The required entrance route, counselling authority, seat categories and score accepted can differ by state, university and institution. Verify that the college and its qualification are recognised through the INC before accepting admission.
bullets: 2026–27 entrance test target date: before 15 June 2026.
2026–27 academic session start: 1 August 2026.
2026–27 last date of admission: 30 September 2026.
Use the relevant state/university notification for applications, counselling and seat allocation.

sections.heading: Next education and career options
paragraphs: M.Sc. Nursing is a direct academic progression. INC lists B.Sc. Nursing, B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing and Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing as acceptable qualifications, along with a minimum 55% aggregate, registration as a nurse and midwife or equivalent with a State Nursing Registration Council, and one year of work experience after Basic B.Sc. Nursing. Universities may add entrance and specialisation-specific requirements.

For employment, graduates commonly pursue staff-nurse and nursing-officer roles after obtaining the registration required by the recruiting organisation. As a dated central-government example, the AIIMS NORCET-6 notice accepted B.Sc. Nursing from an INC/State Nursing Council-recognised institution or university, plus registration as a nurse and midwife. Recruitment rules, vacancies, age limits, experience requirements, examinations and pay change by employer and notification, so the current advertisement governs.
bullets: Postgraduate route: M.Sc. Nursing, subject to INC and university eligibility requirements.
Employment route: staff-nurse and nursing-officer recruitment, normally after required professional registration.
Advanced options can include INC-regulated post-basic specialty diplomas and nurse-practitioner residency programs where their separate eligibility requirements are met.
Check the employer’s current notification rather than relying on an earlier recruitment cycle.
